name: Oat Farls
recipeInstructions:
1. The day before serving, mix the oats with 1-1/4 cups of the buttermilk in a bowl. Cover and let stand overnight at room temperature.
2. Preheat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit. Lightly grease a baking sheet and set aside.
3. Sift together the flour, salt, and baking soda.
4. Gradually beat the flour mixture into the oat-buttermilk mixture, adding remaining buttermilk as you work, to form a soft dough. You may not need to use the full 2-1/4 cups of buttermilk.
5. Turn dough out onto a floured surface and flatten with hands or a floured rolling pin so that it forms a circle about 1 inch thick and 9 to 10 inches in diameter.
6. With a sharp knife, cut into quarters or eighths, as desired, and place on prepared baking sheet.
7. Bake for 40 minutes, or until browned. Serve warm.
Note: These are high in fiber and low in fat, provided you don't slather them with butter. Delicious with jam or marmalade. Yield: 8 servings.
